raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general request to check the passage centrally includes verifying whether the argument moves coherently from interface design and study results to the stated contribution.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims about reformulations, confidence, and satisfaction, so checking whether those claims are supported by the reported study is central.","R1.4":"The passage contains multiple firstness and foundational-contribution claims, making novelty assessment an expected responsibility even if evidence is limited to the artifact.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is broad and the artifact is prose from a paper draft, sentence-level clarity, register, and concision are central expected checks.","RX.1":"Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about which checking tasks are intended and what can be inferred from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong generalization and firstness language, so avoiding exaggerated responsibility or unsupported judgments is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within projection only and not actually edit, summarize, critique, or evaluate the artifact.","RX.4":"Checking research claims can affect downstream scholarly interpretation, so potential harm from unsupported novelty or generalization judgments must be considered.","RX.5":"The projection must remain traceable to explicit cues in the delegation and artifact, especially the empirical claims and contribution language."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general request to check the passage centrally includes verifying whether the argument moves coherently from interface design and study results to the stated contribution.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims about reformulations, confidence, and satisfaction, so checking whether those claims are supported by the reported study is central.
rationale.R1.4: The passage contains multiple firstness and foundational-contribution claims, making novelty assessment an expected responsibility even if evidence is limited to the artifact.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is broad and the artifact is prose from a paper draft, sentence-level clarity, register, and concision are central expected checks.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about which checking tasks are intended and what can be inferred from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong generalization and firstness language, so avoiding exaggerated responsibility or unsupported judgments is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projection only and not actually edit, summarize, critique, or evaluate the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Checking research claims can affect downstream scholarly interpretation, so potential harm from unsupported novelty or generalization judgments must be considered.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must remain traceable to explicit cues in the delegation and artifact, especially the empirical claims and contribution language.
